list.title: module:main/main
list.description:
Electron main process entry point for nero-terminal.

Responsibilities:

  • create the application window — a frameless, Tera Term-style window whose native minimise/maximise/close controls are overlaid at the top-right, leaving room in the custom title bar (to their left) for the in-app Settings button;
  • register the session manager's IPC handlers (see {@link module:main/session-manager});
  • keep the native title-bar overlay colours in step with the renderer's light/dark theme;
  • drive the standard app lifecycle through nero-electron-lib's runApp.

The terminal itself (PTY / SSH backends and the xterm view) lives in the shared libraries under nero_modules/; this file is only the app shell.

list.title: module:main/main~makeWindow
list.description:
Create the application window and wire its lifecycle.

Removes the default menu, stamps the build info into the title (nero-terminal vX.Y.Z [DEBUG] (commit)), configures the frameless title bar with a native-controls overlay, loads the renderer, and disposes the active session when the window closes. Called by runApp on ready and on macOS re-activation.

list.title: module:main/main~parseProfileArg
list.description: Extract a saved-session name from a --open-profile=<name> launch argument.

list.title: module:main/main~sendOpenProfile
list.description: Ask the renderer to open a saved session by name.

list.title: module:main/main~sessions
list.description: The session manager bound to this window. Registered once at startup.

list.title: module:main/main~setupJumpList
list.description: Publish the saved sessions as a Windows taskbar jump list (no-op on other platforms), so right-clicking the taskbar icon can launch a saved session.

list.title: module:main/main~win
list.description: The single application window, or null before creation / after it closes.

list.title: module:main/preload
list.description:
Preload script — the only bridge between the sandboxed renderer and the main process. Runs with Node access but contextIsolation on, and exposes three frozen API objects on window:

  • window.terminalAPI — raw PTY/SSH byte flow (provided by the terminal lib's {@link https://|exposeTerminalBridge}): sendInput, resize, onData, onExit;
  • window.windowAPI — see {@link WindowAPI};
  • window.sessionAPI — see {@link SessionAPI}.

Everything the renderer can ask the main process to do is enumerated here.

list.title: module:main/preload~ProfilesAPI
list.description: Saved-session (PuTTY "Saved Sessions") persistence.

list.title: module:main/preload~SessionAPI
list.description: Session configuration & lifecycle exposed to the renderer.

list.title: module:main/preload~WindowAPI
list.description: Frameless title-bar controls.

list.title: module:main/session-manager
list.description:
Session manager — the PuTTY-style session config & lifecycle for nero-terminal, in the main process.

Opens either a remote SSH session ({@link SshHost} / ssh2) or a local shell ({@link PtyHost} / node-pty); both implement the same host interface, so the same terminal-lib IPC bridge (attachPtyToIpc) serves either backend. Also detects local shells and persists "Saved Sessions" to userData/sessions.json.

Extracted from {@link module:main/main} so it can be exercised headlessly by scripts/session-smoke.js.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~SessionManager
list.description: The object returned by {@link createSessionManager}.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~SessionOptions
list.description: Options accepted by {@link openSession} (and window.sessionAPI.open).

list.title: module:main/session-manager~SessionProfile
list.description: A saved session (PuTTY "Saved Sessions"). A superset of {@link SessionOptions} plus presentation fields; secrets (password, passphrase) are never persisted.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~SessionResult
list.description: Result of {@link openSession}.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~ShellDescriptor
list.description: A local shell offered under the "Local" connection type.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~buildJumpList
list.description: Build a Windows taskbar jump list from saved session profiles, for app.setJumpList(). Each entry relaunches the app with --open-profile="<name>" so the running instance opens that saved session. Returns an empty array when there are no profiles (which clears the list).

list.title: module:main/session-manager~createSessionManager
list.description: Create a session manager bound to a single window. Holds at most one live session (a host + its IPC bridge) at a time.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~createSessionManager~closeSession
list.description: Dispose the active session — the IPC bridge and its backend host, if any.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~createSessionManager~openSession
list.description:
Open a session and wire it to the renderer's terminal via the terminal-lib IPC bridge (attachPtyToIpc). Any existing session is closed first.

For type: 'ssh' a {@link SshHost} is created (a keyPath is read into a private-key buffer here); for type: 'local' a {@link PtyHost} is spawned.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~createSessionManager~register
list.description: Register every renderer-facing IPC handler on ipcMain: session:detectShells, session:browseDir, session:browseKey, session:open, session:close, and profiles:list / profiles:save / profiles:delete. Call once at startup.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~detectShells
list.description: Detect the local shells available on this machine (for the "Local" connection type). On Windows: PowerShell, pwsh 7 (if present), Command Prompt, WSL (if present), Git Bash (if present). Elsewhere: $SHELL plus common alternatives.

list.title: module:main/session-manager~firstExisting
list.description: Return the first path in paths that exists, else null.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer
list.description:
Renderer for nero-terminal's Tera Term-style window.

Architecture:

  • one persistent xterm terminal ({@link module:renderer/renderer~ensureTerminal}) created at startup and always shown, so it always renders (it is never hidden/re-shown — that previously broke xterm's first paint);
  • the session configuration is an overlay dialog over the terminal, shown at startup and reopenable from the title-bar gear button;
  • {@link module:renderer/renderer~startSession} opens a session through window.sessionAPI, then pipes window.terminalAPI bytes into the view;
  • UI text is localised via {@link I18n} (data-i18n attributes + t()), and the light/dark theme uses Bootstrap's data-bs-theme.

The terminal backends (PTY/SSH) and the xterm wrapper come from the shared libraries under nero_modules/.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~applyLiveTerminalOptions
list.description: Apply the current appearance settings (font, cursor, scrollback, colour theme) to the live persistent terminal immediately, without reopening the session — so changes such as a larger font are reflected at once, even mid-session.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~closeSettings
list.description: Hide the configuration overlay and return focus to the terminal.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~ensureTerminal
list.description: Create the single, persistent {@link TerminalView} (xterm + fit + canvas renderer) once and mount it into #terminal. Idempotent. Because the terminal is created while visible and never hidden, xterm always paints correctly.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~flash
list.description: Show a transient message in the dialog footer.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~formToProfile
list.description: Snapshot the configuration form into a {@link SessionProfile}-shaped object, used both for saving and for opening a session.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~init
list.description: Bootstrap the renderer: localise the static DOM and wire the language picker, create the persistent terminal, attach every event handler synchronously (so the buttons work even if async setup is slow or fails), populate the colour-theme and installed-font pickers, show the settings overlay, then load the detected shells and saved sessions.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~openProfileByName
list.description: Load a saved session profile by name and start it immediately. Invoked when a Windows jump-list entry (or a --open-profile launch argument) selects a saved session; shows the settings dialog if the profile is missing.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~openSettings
list.description: Show the session-configuration overlay over the terminal and focus the host field.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~resolveLaunch
list.description: Turn a form snapshot into the payload for window.sessionAPI.open, plus a short display label for the title bar. Throws a localised Error if a required field (SSH host, or a custom command / shell) is missing.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~setUiTheme
list.description: Apply the Bootstrap light/dark UI theme, swap the moon/sun icon, and ask the main process to recolour the native title-bar overlay to match.

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~startDefaultSession
list.description: Open the default local shell immediately, skipping manual configuration. Backs the "open the default terminal at startup" toggle (and is a sensible fallback when a requested profile cannot be opened).

list.title: module:renderer/renderer~startSession
list.description: Open a session for the current form values and connect it to the persistent terminal. Tears down any previous session, applies per-session xterm options (font / theme / scrollback / cursor) and resets the screen, buffers early output until the view is ready, then pipes terminalAPI ⇄ the view and closes the overlay. Validation / connection errors are shown inline via {@link flash}.
